text.title: data-driven-requirements Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for data-driven-requirements as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Heavily-used features look high-priority because users who hated them already churned. Add churn-cohort comparison or you'll over-invest in features your remaining base loves and your lost base rejected.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Page views" and "DAU" feel like business signal but are easy to game. Tie every requirement to a downstream conversion / retention / revenue metric, not a top-of-funnel proxy.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The "current value" cell ages out fast (seasonality, marketing campaigns, releases). Stamp every metric with as_of_date and a query/dashboard URL; reject baselines older than ~30 days for active surfaces.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A/B plans that don't pre-calculate MDE (minimum detectable effect) and required N produce "no significant difference" results that are actually underpowered. Use a power calculator before, not after.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Aggregate metrics improve while every segment gets worse (or vice versa). Always slice by acquisition channel, plan tier, geo, and device class before declaring a baseline.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: PII or behavioral data may be unusable for product analytics under GDPR/CCPA without a lawful basis. Requirements built on data you cannot legally use will fail at the legal review gate.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: "Users who use feature X retain 2x" almost never means "shipping feature X for everyone doubles retention". Self-selection swallows the effect. Frame requirements around hypotheses to be tested, not foregone conclusions.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: 95% of enterprise AI projects fail without a data strategy. The framework asks for the four impact areas — most teams fill three with guesses; mark unknowns as "to be measured" not as point estimates.
